title: Earthly, Flaky German Pistachio Dumplings
description: A traditional German-inspired breakfast dish with an earthy and nutty twist, featuring pistachio-studded dumplings served with a side of garlic-infused mushroom sauce.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine almond flour, pistachios, salt, and nutmeg.
Add beaten eggs to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a floured surface and knead for 5 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Roll out each ball of dough into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
In a large skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of butter over medium heat.
Add sliced mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they release their moisture and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook for an additional minute.
Place a dumpling in the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Transfer the dumpling to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper and repeat with the remaining dough.
Bake the dumplings in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Meanwhile, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add sour cream and grated Parmesan cheese to the saucepan and stir until smooth.
Add chopped chives to the saucepan and season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the dumplings hot with a side of garlic-infused mushroom sauce.
